preamble: The spontaneous diphthongization of the low vowel 'a' into the rising diphthong 'ie' is a hallmark of several Romance language developments, particularly in stressed open syllables.

examples.note: Latin [a] in stressed open syllables diphthongized to [ie] in Old French.
While the source is often a mid-vowel, the process reflects a broader trend of spontaneous diphthongization in the Romance family.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
